page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 56 vCPUs mapped to 28 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The instance provides 224 GB of dedicated system memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and does not include bundled local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities. The instance scores in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory performance is highly competitive, with PassMark memory scores reaching the top 10% tier and small-block read-only memory bandwidth placing in the top 25%. However, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the average category. Cryptographic and vector operations are supported via hardware flags including AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and AVX.

Given its high core count and balanced memory-to-core ratio, this instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations, all of which achieved strong benchmark ratings. It also demonstrates capable performance for small-scale language model inference, particularly in text generation. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high resource density provide a stable, predictable platform for parallelized enterprise workloads without the overhead of GPU accelerators.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 56 vCPUs and 224 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 28 physical cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance offers dedicated CPU allocation with 4.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly efficient, with PassMark memory scores reaching the top 10% tier. The instance is optimized for parallel processing tasks, including software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations. It also delivers strong performance for small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a compute-optimized instance with 56 vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring fifty-six vCPUs and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for demanding parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 56 vCPUs and 224 GB of RAM powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
Dedicated CPU allocation with 28 physical cores and multithreading
Top 10% memory performance in PassMark benchmarks
Strong multi-core execution for software compilation and ray tracing
Suitable for database operations and small-scale LLM inference
